methodology.source: self-measured from the session transcript (transcripts/66eb5ae0-849c-40cd-893e-c13a1ed004c6.jsonl); per-message `usage` fields summed across the whole session
caveats: This bookkeeping step and the final message are necessarily excluded from their own totals (usage recorded after the measurement snapshot cannot be counted).
Tool result token weights are approximations (chars/4); all other figures are measured values from the transcript.
The transcript is the authoritative source for verification.

session.subagents: none spawned (background bash tasks and monitors only, which log into the same session transcript)

by_phase.phase: 5. Fly.io setup, GitHub repo, CI authoring, first deploy + smoke

waste_analysis.note: ~2h25m spent diagnosing startup_failures and waiting out a GitHub-wide Actions incident; includes probe-workflow isolation, repeated dispatch retries, and monitor wakeups. Externally caused; the diagnosis (trivial probe also startup-failing, status page confirmation) was necessary to rule out a workflow defect.

Chaos run flagged a violation that turned out to be prior-run volume residue read at run start, not a cluster bug; included per-key bisection, one silently-dying 90s run (suspected checker OOM), the reset-phase fix, and re-verification. Produced a real harness improvement, but roughly half the tokens were spent on the misdiagnosis path.

waste_analysis.segment: Repo remote confusion + GitHub API 503 retries
waste_analysis.note: Push initially went to the wrong remote (pre-existing origin overlooked), then secret-setting and run-listing hit repeated 503s; resolved with remote restructuring and retry loops.
